text: Use bash to run exactly: curl -sS --max-time 5 -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://example.com . Report the actual exit status and HTTP code or exact refusal. Run nothing else.
Permission denied: Network command blocked under --no-network: curl -sS --max-time 5 -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://example.com

content.text:
**Exact result:**

- **Exit status:** Command was not executed — blocked before any process was spawned.
- **HTTP code:** None returned.
- **Refusal message (verbatim):**
  > `Permission denied: Network command blocked under --no-network: curl -sS --max-time 5 -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://example.com`

This agent is running with the `--no-network` flag active, which intercepts and blocks outbound network commands such as `curl`. No HTTP request was made and no HTTP status code was obtained.

content.text:
**Exact refusal from the sandbox:**

> `Permission denied: Network command blocked under --no-network: curl -sS --max-time 5 -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://example.com`

- **Exit status:** The tool layer intercepted the call before the shell executed it — no OS-level exit code was produced.
- **HTTP code:** None — the request never reached the network.
- **Reason:** This environment is running with the `--no-network` flag, which blocks outbound network commands (including `curl`) at the agent sandbox level.
